div.h3: Per-slot lifecycle in MPMC ring buffer

p.em: Technical Specification: Intel manual grade. Reference: Zero-Copy Message Bus.

g.title: ### Invariants - Slot is ready for a producer. - `sequence_number` matches the `buffer_index`. - Buffer memory is owned by the Ring.
### Invariants - Producer successfully performed CAS on tail. - Data is being copied via memcpy/zero-copy pointer. - Sequence number remains `pos` until commit.
### Invariants - Producer finished write. - Atomic store performed on `seq` with `memory_order_release`. - Data is visible to consumers.
### Invariants - Consumer successfully performed CAS on head. - Data is being read from the buffer slot. - Sequence number remains `pos+1`.
### Invariants - Consumer finished read. - Atomic store performed on `seq` to signal reclamation. - Slot cannot be reused until `pos` increments per lap.

ul.li: Memory Order: All store(seq, ...) operations must use memory_order_release.
Atomic Ops: CAS operations use memory_order_acquire_release on ring head/tail.
Avoidance of ABA: 64-bit sequence numbers provide immunity to ABA for hardware lifespan.
pos: Logical index of the slot (0 to capacity-1).
seq: Atomic value stored in slot metadata.
cap: Total buffer capacity (must be power of 2 for masking).

div.p: text [ +0] Atomic64 Sequence Number (Metadata) [ +8] Cache Line Padding (64B alignment) [+64] Data Payload (Variable Size)

g.title: ### Invariants - Slot is ready for a producer. - `sequence_number` matches the `buffer_index`. - Buffer memory is owned by the Ring.
### Invariants - Producer successfully performed CAS on tail. - Data is being copied via memcpy/zero-copy pointer. - Sequence number remains `pos` until commit.
### Invariants - Producer finished write. - Atomic store performed on `seq` with `memory_order_release`. - Data is visible to consumers.
### Invariants - Consumer successfully performed CAS on head. - Data is being read from the buffer slot. - Sequence number remains `pos+1`.
### Invariants - Consumer finished read. - Atomic store performed on `seq` to signal reclamation. - Slot cannot be reused until `pos` increments per lap.
